story.iWant: to extract and display all preset parameters as structured JSON
story.soThat: I can analyze color science data, validate conversions programmatically, and learn how presets work internally
story.tasks:
### Task 1: Create inspect CLI Command (AC-1, AC-5) - [ ] 1.1 Create cmd/cli/inspect.go file - [ ] 1.2 Implement runInspect() command handler - [ ] 1.3 Add command flags (--output, --pretty) - [ ] 1.4 Register command with root - [ ] 1.5 Add unit tests for CLI command
### Task 2: Implement inspect Package (AC-1, AC-2) - [ ] 2.1 Create internal/inspect/inspect.go file - [ ] 2.2 Define InspectOutput struct - [ ] 2.3 Implement ToJSON() function - [ ] 2.4 Implement ToJSONWithMetadata() function - [ ] 2.5 Add version injection at build time
### Task 3: Format Support (AC-3) - [ ] 3.1 Integrate with existing parsers - [ ] 3.2 Test all three formats (NP3, XMP, lrtemplate) - [ ] 3.3 Add table-driven tests with all 1,479 sample files
### Task 4: Output File Handling (AC-4) - [ ] 4.1 Implement --output flag logic in runInspect() - [ ] 4.2 Add file write logic with directory creation - [ ] 4.3 Handle file write errors - [ ] 4.4 Test file output (path, permissions, overwrite)
### Task 5: Error Handling (AC-6) - [ ] 5.1 Implement format detection error handling - [ ] 5.2 Implement parse error handling with ConversionError - [ ] 5.3 Implement file read error handling - [ ] 5.4 Set exit codes (0=success, 1=error) - [ ] 5.5 Add error tests
### Task 6: Performance Testing (AC-7) - [ ] 6.1 Create internal/inspect/inspect_test.go file - [ ] 6.2 Implement benchmark tests - [ ] 6.3 Run benchmarks and verify <50ms target - [ ] 6.4 Add performance regression tests - [ ] 6.5 Profile memory usage (<10 MB target)
### Task 7: Integration and Documentation - [ ] 7.1 Update main.go to register inspect command - [ ] 7.2 Update README with inspect examples - [ ] 7.3 Update help text - [ ] 7.4 Add to Makefile - [ ] 7.5 End-to-end testing

story-context.acceptanceCriteria:
### AC-1: Inspect Command Outputs Valid JSON - recipe inspect FILE outputs complete parameter set as valid JSON to stdout - JSON is parseable by standard JSON parsers (jq, Python json module, JavaScript JSON.parse) - All UniversalRecipe fields present in output (50+ parameters) - Output is pretty-printed with indentation for human readability (2-space indent) - JSON structure follows standard conventions (camelCase keys, consistent types)
### AC-2: Metadata Wrapper Included - JSON output includes metadata section with source file info - Metadata contains: source_file, source_format, parsed_at (ISO 8601), recipe_version - Timestamp is UTC timezone - Recipe version matches go build -ldflags injected version string - Metadata section is separate from parameters (top-level keys)
### AC-3: All Formats Supported - Inspect command successfully parses and outputs JSON for NP3 files - Inspect command successfully parses and outputs JSON for XMP files - Inspect command successfully parses and outputs JSON for lrtemplate files - No format-specific errors when used correctly - Parameter coverage matches each format's capabilities
### AC-4: Output Flag Saves to File - --output FILE flag writes JSON to specified file instead of stdout - File created with correct permissions (0644 - rw-r--r--) - Overwrites existing files without confirmation (standard CLI behavior) - Creates parent directories if needed (mkdir -p behavior) - Success message printed to stderr after file written
### AC-5: Format Auto-Detection - Inspect command automatically detects input file format from extension - No manual format flag required (uses detectFormat() from converter) - Clear error if format cannot be detected - Error message lists supported formats (.np3, .xmp, .lrtemplate) - Unknown extensions rejected with helpful message
### AC-6: Error Handling - Parse errors return ConversionError type from Epic 1 - Error message includes: operation (parse), format, underlying cause - Invalid file format errors are user-friendly (no technical jargon) - File read errors clearly identify the problematic file path - Exit codes follow Unix conventions (0 = success, 1 = error)
### AC-7: Performance Requirement - Inspect command completes in <50ms for typical preset files (<50KB) - JSON serialization completes in <5ms (Go json.MarshalIndent is fast) - Total user experience (file read + parse + JSON + output) <100ms - Memory usage <10 MB (single UniversalRecipe in memory) - Benchmark tests validate performance targets

doc.title: Product Requirements Document
doc.snippet: Recipe enables advanced users to extract and display all preset parameters as structured JSON for programmatic analysis, debugging, and learning color science internals

doc.title: Epic 5 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: inspect command outputs valid, parseable JSON for all supported formats with metadata wrapper including source file info, format, timestamp, and Recipe version

doc.title: Architecture Documentation
doc.snippet: All CLI commands use Cobra framework with consistent flag patterns, automatic format detection, and ConversionError type from Epic 1

doc.title: Architecture Documentation
doc.snippet: UniversalRecipe is the universal data structure with JSON struct tags already defined - serialization is automatic using json.MarshalIndent()

doc.title: Story 3-1: Cobra CLI Structure
doc.snippet: Commands use cobra.Command with Use, Short, Long, Args (ExactArgs), and RunE. Flags use StringP, Bool patterns. Error handling returns errors to Cobra for exit code 1

artifact.reason: Core data model that inspect command serializes to JSON. Already has JSON struct tags for all 50+ fields. No modifications needed.
Example CLI command structure to follow for inspect command. Shows Cobra pattern, flag handling, detectFormat usage, error handling, and logging patterns
Root command where inspect command will be registered. Shows global flags (--verbose, --json), version handling, and logger initialization
NP3 parser that returns *models.UniversalRecipe. Reused by inspect command without modification
XMP parser that returns *models.UniversalRecipe. Reused by inspect command without modification
lrtemplate parser that returns *models.UniversalRecipe. Reused by inspect command without modification
Error type used for all conversion failures. Inspect command will use this for parse errors with operation context

package.name: github.com/spf13/cobra
package.reason: CLI framework for inspect command structure

package.name: encoding/json
package.reason: JSON serialization with MarshalIndent for pretty printing

package.name: os
package.reason: File I/O (ReadFile, WriteFile) for input/output handling

package.name: path/filepath
package.reason: Path manipulation for output directory creation

story-context.constraints: - MUST reuse existing Epic 1 parsers (np3.Parse, xmp.Parse, lrtemplate.Parse) without modification - MUST follow Cobra CLI patterns established in Epic 3 (cmd/cli/convert.go as reference) - MUST use ConversionError type from internal/converter/error.go for all parse errors - MUST use json.MarshalIndent() from stdlib (no external JSON libraries) - MUST output valid, parseable JSON that works with standard tools (jq, Python json module) - MUST inject version at build time using go build -ldflags pattern - MUST create new internal/inspect/ package (do NOT modify existing Epic 1 code) - MUST follow Unix conventions: exit code 0 = success, 1 = error, stdout for output, stderr for messages - MUST handle file write errors gracefully with clear error messages - MUST create parent directories when using --output flag (os.MkdirAll pattern) - NO framework dependencies beyond Cobra (already in use from Epic 3) - NO modifications to UniversalRecipe struct (JSON tags already defined) - NO new parsers (purely presentation layer on existing conversion engine)

interface.name: ToJSONWithMetadata
interface.signature: func ToJSONWithMetadata(recipe *models.UniversalRecipe, sourceFile, format string) ([]byte, error)

interface.name: InspectOutput
interface.signature: type InspectOutput struct { Metadata struct { SourceFile string, SourceFormat string, ParsedAt string, RecipeVersion string }, Parameters *models.UniversalRecipe }

tests.standards: Go testing framework with table-driven tests. All tests in *_test.go files. Benchmarks use testing.B for performance validation. Integration tests use testdata/ fixtures from Epic 1 (1,479 sample files: 22 NP3, 913 XMP, 544 lrtemplate).

locations.location: internal/inspect/inspect_test.go - Unit tests for ToJSON, ToJSONWithMetadata
testdata/np3/*.np3 - NP3 sample files for format testing
testdata/xmp/*.xmp - XMP sample files for format testing
testdata/lrtemplate/*.lrtemplate - lrtemplate sample files for format testing

ideas.idea: TestToJSON_AllFields - Verify all 50+ UniversalRecipe fields present in JSON output using reflection to check struct tags
TestToJSON_ValidJSON - Parse output with json.Unmarshal to confirm valid JSON structure
TestToJSON_PrettyPrint - Verify 2-space indentation in output using string analysis
TestToJSONWithMetadata - Verify metadata section contains all 4 required fields (source_file, source_format, parsed_at, recipe_version)
TestMetadata_ISO8601 - Verify parsed_at timestamp is valid ISO 8601 format (time.Parse with RFC3339)
TestInspect_AllFormats - Table-driven test with NP3, XMP, lrtemplate samples, verify JSON output for each
TestInspect_SampleFiles - Run inspect on all 1,479 sample files, verify valid JSON for each (may be slow, mark as integration test)
TestInspect_OutputFile - Verify --output flag creates file at correct path with 0644 permissions
TestInspect_OutputDirectoryCreation - Test parent directory creation when path doesn't exist
TestInspect_Overwrite - Verify overwrite behavior (no confirmation, just overwrites)
TestDetectFormat - Verify auto-detection works for .np3, .xmp, .lrtemplate extensions (may already exist in Epic 3)
TestInspect_InvalidFormat - Test error message when unknown format (.txt, .jpg, etc.)
TestInspect_ParseError - Verify ConversionError type returned for corrupt files
TestInspect_FileNotFound - Verify clear error for missing files
TestInspect_ExitCodes - Verify exit code 0 on success, 1 on error (integration test)
BenchmarkInspectJSON - Measure ToJSON performance, target <5ms for serialization
BenchmarkInspectEndToEnd - Measure full workflow (read + parse + JSON), target <50ms
TestInspect_MemoryUsage - Profile memory with testing.AllocsPerRun, verify <10 MB
